subject: [PATCH man-pages v1 0/6] userfaultfd: document read-write protect mode

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mike Rapoport found the documentation content acceptable, but left the formatting and organization to another person, Alex.
reviews.raw_body:
The content of the docs looks fine to me.

Leaving groff and organization to Alex :)

Acked-by: Mike Rapoport (Microsoft) <rppt@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary:
Reviewer pointed out that the documentation for UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP does not mention the requirement to hold the userfaultfd file descriptor open while calling ioctl(2) to install or remove read-write protection.

Reviewer Alejandro Colomar responded positively to the patch, thanking the submitter and wishing them a good day.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Mike,

On 2026-05-26T12:30:58+0300, Mike Rapoport wrote:

---

Thanks!  :)


Have a lovely day!
Alex

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch series documents the userfaultfd read-write protect mode, a working-set tracking mechanism for VM guest memory introduced in Linux 7.2. The patches add new UAPI documentation for various ioctls and features related to this mode, including UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP, UFFD_FEATURE_RWP, and UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC. The series also updates the userfaultfd manual page to include an overview of the read-write protect mode.
